preamble: Uvularization of the alveolar trill [r] to the uvular fricative [ʁ] is a major phonetic shift that characterizes the 'European Guttural R'. It spread across several European languages starting in the 17th century.

examples.note: Shift from alveolar trill to uvular fricative/approximant in Standard French.
Alveolar [r] remains in some dialects (e.g., Bavarian), but Standard German has shifted to uvular [ʁ] or trill [ʀ].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Lass, R. (1997). Historical Linguistics and Language Change.
